FUNC-001.syntactical_description: Fails to conduct current through the power contacts
Conducts current at insufficient capacity due to degraded contact surface
Intermittently conducts current through the power contacts
Uncommanded conducting of current through power contacts
Conducts current too late after coil energization command
Fails to cease conducting current when coil is de-energized
Erratically conducts current with unstable contact resistance

FUNC-002.syntactical_description: Fails to generate magnetic force to close the contactor
Generates insufficient magnetic force to fully seat the armature
Intermittently generates magnetic force due to coil connection fault
Uncommanded coil generates magnetic force without control signal
Generates magnetic force too late after voltage is applied to coil
Fails to cease generate magnetic force when supply is removed
Erratically generates magnetic force causing armature chatter

FUNC-003.syntactical_description: Fails to signal contact status to the control circuit
Signals contact status with degraded accuracy or insufficient current capacity
Intermittently signals contact status due to auxiliary contact bounce
Uncommanded signaling of contact status without main contact state change
Signals contact status too late relative to actual main contact position
Fails to cease signaling contact status when main contacts open
Erratically signals contact status with corrupted position indication

FUNC-004.syntactical_description: Fails to sense overload current through the bimetallic element
Senses overload current with insufficient sensitivity due to degraded bimetallic calibration
Intermittently senses overload current due to thermal drift
Uncommanded sense of overload current triggers nuisance trip at normal load
Senses overload current too late allowing motor thermal damage
Fails to cease sense overload current after trip and reset
Erratically senses overload current with unstable trip threshold

FUNC-005.syntactical_description: Fails to disconnect motor circuit during overload condition
Disconnects motor circuit with insufficient speed causing degraded protection
Intermittently disconnects motor circuit with unreliable trip actuation
Uncommanded disconnecting of motor circuit without overload present
Disconnects motor circuit too early before actual overload threshold is reached
Fails to cease disconnecting motor circuit preventing manual reset
Erratically disconnects motor circuit with unpredictable trip behavior

FUNC-006.syntactical_description: Fails to signal trip status to the monitoring system
Signals trip status with insufficient reliability due to degraded contact surface
Intermittently signals trip status with unstable auxiliary contact
Uncommanded signaling of trip status without actual overload trip
Signals trip status too late after mechanical trip has occurred
Fails to cease signaling trip status after relay is reset
Erratically signals trip status with corrupted indication

FUNC-007.syntactical_description: Fails to switch motor circuit between ON and OFF positions
Switches motor circuit with insufficient contact pressure causing degraded performance
Intermittently switches motor circuit due to worn detent mechanism
Uncommanded switching of motor circuit from vibration or shock
Switches motor circuit with delayed mechanical response
Fails to cease switching motor circuit when moved to OFF position
Erratically switches motor circuit with unreliable position detent

FUNC-008.syntactical_description: Fails to limit overload current allowing unprotected motor operation
Limits overload current at insufficient accuracy due to degraded thermal calibration
Intermittently limits overload current with inconsistent trip response
Uncommanded limiting of overload current causes nuisance trip at normal load
Limits overload current too late exceeding the Class 10 tripping curve
Fails to cease limiting overload current preventing motor restart after reset
Erratically limits overload current with unstable trip threshold

FUNC-009.syntactical_description: Fails to interrupt short-circuit current during fault condition
Interrupts short-circuit current with insufficient speed causing degraded protection
Intermittently interrupts short-circuit current with unreliable magnetic trip
Uncommanded interrupting of short-circuit current during normal inrush
Interrupts short-circuit current too late allowing equipment damage
Fails to cease interrupting short-circuit current preventing circuit re-energization
Erratically interrupts short-circuit current with unpredictable trip threshold
